title: Thorough SAFETY comments on every unsafe block — should be defended if anyone proposes removal
description: Every unsafe block in device.rs, key.rs, and connection.rs has a SAFETY comment explaining the invariant being upheld. This is correctly required for Rust unsafe code and above the baseline for most FFI crates. A future contributor might suggest removing them as 'obvious'. They should be kept: the invariants (zeroed initial state, stable address from Box, lifetime bounds on dev_ptr) are non-obvious to someone unfamiliar with wolfTPM's initialization contract.
